title: T-050 — Seed scanner.* permissions onto roles (NNN-06-scanner-p1-permission-seed.sql)
prompt:
# T-050 — Seed scanner.* permissions onto roles (NNN-06-scanner-p1-permission-seed.sql)

## Goal
Add an idempotent SQL seed migration that GRANTS the 10 `scanner.*` permission strings (added by T-049) to the org roles named in the PRD RBAC matrix, so the live click-through (DoD) does not 403. Closes cross-cutting audit finding X-1 (RBAC unseeded).

## Implementation contract
- New migration `NNN-06-scanner-p1-permission-seed.sql` numbered contiguously ABOVE the current HEAD (runner regex `^(\d{3})-[a-z0-9-]+\.sql$`; pick the next free number ≥ HEAD at run start so it is not silently skipped — the 01-npd deploy gotcha).
- Idempotent `INSERT INTO public.role_permissions (role_id, permission) ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`, org-scoped, including the Apex bootstrap org.
- Resolve role_id by role key/name (do NOT hardcode role UUIDs).
- Grant matrix derived from PRD `§12.5` RBAC table: least-privilege per role; org-admin role family receives the full `scanner.*` set (mirror migration 149 org-admin pattern).
- Wire the module's nav `permission_key` in `apps/web/lib/navigation/module-registry.ts` to the base read permission if it is currently null, so nav RBAC gating is no longer a no-op.

## Acceptance criteria (Given-When-Then)
- AC1 — Given the migration runs, when role_permissions is queried for a fresh org, then each of the 10 `scanner.*` permissions is granted to at least the PRD `§12.5` roles, and org-admin holds all 10.
- AC2 — Given a seeded least-privilege role, when it hits a granted action, then allowed; when it hits an ungranted elevated action, then 403 (reachability proven both directions, integration test on real local Postgres).
- AC3 — Given the migration re-runs, when applied twice, then it is a no-op (idempotent).
- AC4 — Given `assertPermission`/`requirePermission` path, then it returns allow for granted, Forbidden for ungranted; `pnpm db:test` green; RLS unaffected.

## Test strategy (RED first)
1. Add an integration test asserting grant/deny both directions for a seeded role on real local Postgres (`pnpm db:up && pnpm db:test`) — RED before the migration.
2. Add the seed migration; re-run — GREEN.
3. Re-run the migration to prove idempotency.

## Risk red lines
- Grants MUST be in a DB migration (reach Supabase on deploy), not app-code only.
- Do NOT over-grant: respect PRD `§12.5` least-privilege; only org-admin gets the full set; no write perms to viewer/read-only roles.
- Migration number must be ≥ current HEAD and contiguous (avoid the silent-never-runs gotcha — X-2).
- org_id Wave0 lock; `app.current_org_id()` scoping; no hardcoded role UUIDs.

## Closeout evidence required
- changed_files (with line counts)
- exact RED→GREEN test commands and outputs
- acceptance_criteria_status (one row per AC)
- deviations_from_prd (or "none")
- git_status

pipeline_inputs.description: Add an idempotent SQL seed migration that GRANTS the 10 `scanner.*` permission strings (added by T-049) to the org roles named in the PRD RBAC matrix. Closes cross-cutting finding X-1 (RBAC unseeded): module adds permission enum strings but no migration grants them to roles, so every page builds green then 403s on the live deploy. Models on packages/db/migrations/146-npd-allergen-write-permission-seed.sql and 148-settings-infra-permission-seed.sql (idempotent INSERT INTO role_permissions ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING).
pipeline_inputs.details: Seed role_permissions grants for the 10 `scanner.*` strings per PRD §12.5 role matrix. Pattern: idempotent INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, org-scoped (incl. Apex bootstrap org), role_id resolved by role key (no hardcoded UUIDs). org-admin family gets full set (mirror mig 149). Migration numbered ≥ HEAD contiguous (X-2). Source: sidecar CONSOLIDATED-FINDINGS X-1 + proposed-tasks stub for 06-scanner-p1.
out_of_scope: Do not add/rename permission enum strings (that is the enum task).
Do not grant write/elevated perms to viewer/read-only roles.

cross_module_dependencies.reason: Settings owns the permission enum + role_permissions schema (mig 080). Seed inserts into that table.
app.current_org_id() / withOrgContext used for org-scoped grant resolution and the grant/deny integration test.

acceptance_criteria: Migration grants each of the 10 scanner.* permissions to the PRD §12.5 roles; org-admin holds all 10.
A seeded least-privilege role is allowed on a granted action and 403 on an ungranted elevated action (integration test, real Postgres).
pnpm db:test green; RLS unaffected; nav permission_key wired if previously null.
test_strategy: RED: integration test asserting grant/deny both directions for a seeded role on real local Postgres before the migration.
GREEN: add the seed migration; re-run.
Re-run the migration to prove idempotency.
risk_red_lines: Grants must be in a DB migration, not app-code only.
Respect PRD least-privilege; only org-admin gets the full set.
Migration number ≥ HEAD and contiguous (X-2 silent-never-runs gotcha).
org_id Wave0 lock; app.current_org_id() scoping; no hardcoded role UUIDs.
